description:
IF  cumulative charge throughput exceeds design cycle limit
THEN
  · BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag := 1
  · Trigger SOH re-calibration request
supplementary_info:
Function: SOC/SOH Estimation
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring

missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit, resulting in BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag being set to 1 and an SOH re-calibration request being triggered.
Test the boundary condition where cumulative charge throughput is at or very close to but not exceeding the design cycle limit, ensuring that BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag remains unset.
Test a scenario where an error in cumulative charge throughput calculation occurs, and verify that BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag does not get triggered incorrectly.
Verify the state transition from normal operation to fault condition when cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit.

cases.title: Test the normal behavior where cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit, resulting in BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag being set to 1 and an SOH re-calibration request being triggered.
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS correctly sets the BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag to 1 and triggers an SOH re-calibration request when cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.
cases.postcondition: System returned to normal operating state.

steps.action: Simulate a condition where cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit
Wait for [NEEDS REVIEW] duration to allow BMS processing
Verify that BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag is set to 1
Verify that SOH re-calibration request is triggered

cases.raw_html:
<testcase>
<title>Test the normal behavior where cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit, resulting in BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag being set to 1 and an SOH re-calibration request being triggered.</title>
<objective>To verify that the BMS correctly sets the BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag to 1 and triggers an SOH re-calibration request when cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit.</objective>
<precondition>BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.</precondition>
<steps>
<step order="1">
<action>Simulate a condition where cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit</action>
<expected>null</expected>
</step>
<step order="2">
<action>Wait for [NEEDS REVIEW] duration to allow BMS processing</action>
<expected>null</expected>
</step>
<step order="3">
<action>Verify that BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag is set to 1</action>
<expected>BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag := 1</expected>
</step>
<step order="4">
<action>Verify that SOH re-calibration request is triggered</action>
<expected>[NEEDS REVIEW]_SOH_ReCal_Request_Broadcasted := true</expected>
</step>
</steps>
<postcondition>System returned to normal operating state.</postcondition>
</testcase>

description:
While BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 1:
  · Discharge power limited to 30 % (voltage-based estimation)
  · Charging shall be inhibited
  · IF fault persists for > 5 s
    THEN BMS_SafeState_Entry := 1
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault

analysis.direction: Inhibiting charging and limiting discharge power are expected to trigger the entry into a safe state if the fault persists.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test normal behavior where BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag is set and discharge power is limited to 30% while charging is inhibited.
Test the boundary condition for fault persistence duration, ensuring that BMS_SafeState_Entry is triggered exactly after 5 seconds of continuous fault detection.
Verify that BMS_SafeState_Entry is set if the fault persists beyond 5 seconds while discharge power remains limited and charging is inhibited.
Test state transition from normal operation to safe state when the fault condition persists for more than 5 seconds.

cases.title: Test BMS behavior when BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag is set and discharge power is limited to 30% while charging is inhibited
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS limits discharge power to 30% and inhibits charging when BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag is set, and enters a safe state after the fault persists for more than 5 seconds.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.
cases.postcondition: System returned to normal operating state.

steps.action: Set BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 1
steps.expected: BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag := 1; Discharge power limited to 30%; Charging inhibited; [NEEDS REVIEW]

steps.action: Wait for t_Fault_Persistence := 5 s
Verify BMS response after fault persists for more than 5 seconds

description:

  · IF voltage continues to fall towards 2.50 V
    THEN BMS_CellUV_L2_Flag shall be set
supplementary_info:
Function: Undervoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a triggering behavior. The BMS_CellUV_L2_Flag shall be set if the voltage continues to fall towards 2.50 V while BMS_CellUV_Flag is already active and BMS_CellUV_L2_Flag is not yet set.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

description:
IF  maximum cell temperature delta within pack > 15 C
THEN
  · BMS_TempImbalance_Flag := 1
  · Send DTC P0A0A to VCU
supplementary_info:
Function: Thermal Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance
Context: Temperature imbalance monitoring detects cooling system faults or uneven cell aging by tracking the maximum temperature spread across the pack. When imbalance is present alongside an over-temperature condition, cooling is prioritised to the hottest zone:
- BMS_TempImbalance_Flag = 1: max delta > 15 C
- DTC P0A0A sent to VCU on fault

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_TempImbalance_Flag := 1 when maximum cell temperature delta > 15 C; sending DTC P0A0A to VCU on fault.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where BMS_TempImbalance_Flag is set to 1 and DTC P0A0A is sent when the maximum cell temperature delta exceeds 15 C.
Test the boundary condition where the maximum cell temperature delta is exactly 15 C, ensuring no false triggering of BMS_TempImbalance_Flag.
Test the fault scenario where a value slightly above 15 C triggers BMS_TempImbalance_Flag and DTC P0A0A sending to VCU.
Verify that BMS_TempImbalance_Flag remains set to 1 after it has been triggered by a temperature delta of more than 15 C, until the condition is no longer met.
Ensure that the system correctly identifies and logs the maximum cell temperature delta when it exceeds 15 C for fault diagnosis purposes.

description:
Upon BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, the BMS shall:
  · Broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity
  · Reduce charge current target to 50 % of nominal
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating
Context: When a confirmed overvoltage fault is present, the BMS initiates a controlled derating phase before resorting to contactor-level actions. Warning signals are broadcast on CAN for VCU coordination:
- BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1: overvoltage confirmed (BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1)
  Transmission: CAN ID 0x180, periodicity 100 ms
- BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1: charge termination requested
- Charge current target reduced to 50 % of nominal during derating

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1 triggers BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 and Charge_current_target reduction.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions from 0 to 1, triggering the broadcast of BMS_CellOV_Warn and reduction in charge current target.
Test the boundary condition where BMS_CellOV_Flag remains at 1 for exactly 100 ms before transitioning back to 0, ensuring no false warnings are generated.
Test the state transition from normal operation to derating mode when BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1 and verify that BMS_ChargeTerm_Req is set appropriately.
Verify that BMS_CellOV_Warn is correctly broadcast on CAN ID 0x180 at the specified periodicity of 100 ms when BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1.

description:
Upon VCU_HVConnect_Req = 1 with all preconditions satisfied:
  · Close negative main contactor
  · Enable precharge circuit
  · BMS_SystemState := PrechargeActive
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization > 13.2 HV Activation
Context: HV activation follows a precharge sequence to safely energise the DC link before closing the positive contactor, preventing inrush current damage. The sequence is aborted if the bus voltage does not rise within the expected time. Up to 2 retries are permitted before locking out HV activation until power cycle:
- Preconditions: VCU_HVConnect_Req = 1, no active faults, isolation OK, contactors Open, pack voltage in range
- Success criteria: DC bus >= 90 % of pack voltage within precharge timeout
- Failure: BMS_SystemState = HV_Failure, BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag = 1

missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

description:
IF  charge current <= 3 A AND max cell voltage >= 4.18 V
THEN
  · BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag := 1
  · Terminate charge session
supplementary_info:
Function: Charge Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination
Context: Charge termination is triggered by normal completion criteria (current taper indicating full charge), external requests, or fault conditions from other protection functions (e.g., overvoltage from section 1). A charge event record is stored for diagnostic and lifecycle tracking:
- BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag = 1: charge current <= 3 A AND max cell >= 4.18 V
- BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1: termination requested (from charge complete or section 1)
- Recorded data: final SOC, energy transferred, session duration

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag is set to 1 when the charge current <= 3 A and max cell voltage >= 4.18 V.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test that BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag is set to 1 when the charge current is <= 3 A and max cell voltage is >= 4.18 V.
Test that BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag does not trigger if the charge current is exactly 3 A but max cell voltage is below 4.18 V.
Test that BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag does not trigger if the charge current is above 3 A and max cell voltage is >= 4.18 V.
Test that BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag does not trigger if the charge current is <= 3 A but max cell voltage is below 4.18 V due to a sensor fault or other anomaly.

description:
After both BMS_ChargeOC_Flag = 0 and BMS_DischargeOC_Flag = 0:
  · Power limit shall be maintained for 2 s hold time
  · Power shall ramp back to nominal at max 10 % per second
  · A new overcurrent event during ramp shall reset the hold timer
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a behavior where the power limit is maintained for a hold time, and then ramps back to nominal. A new overcurrent event resets the hold timer.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of maintaining power limit for 2 seconds after both BMS_ChargeOC_Flag and BMS_DischargeOC_Flag are cleared.
Verify that the power starts ramping back to nominal at exactly 10% per second when the hold time is met.
Test what happens if a new overcurrent event occurs during the ramp down period and ensure it resets the hold timer.
Check the state transition from power limit maintenance to nominal power level after 2 seconds of hold time.

description:
IF  measured isolation resistance < r_Insulation_MinThreshold
THEN
  · BMS_InsulationFault_Flag := 1
  · Open both main contactors
supplementary_info:
Function: Insulation & HVIL
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring
Context: Isolation resistance monitoring is the primary safety mechanism for detecting insulation degradation between the HV power system and vehicle chassis. The measurement is performed continuously and a fault triggers immediate contactor opening. If the fault persists, it is latched and requires a diagnostic clear to recover:
- BMS_InsulationFault_Flag = 1: isolation resistance < r_Insulation_MinThreshold, contactors open
- BMS_InsulationFault_Latch = 1: fault persisted >= 1 s, latched until diagnostic clear

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_InsulationFault_Flag is set when the measured isolation resistance < r_Insulation_MinThreshold. Latched state persists for 1 second.
missing_critical_info: none

case_intents.description: Test normal operation where BMS_InsulationFault_Flag is not triggered when measured isolation resistance is above r_Insulation_MinThreshold.
Test the boundary condition where measured isolation resistance equals r_Insulation_MinThreshold, ensuring BMS_InsulationFault_Flag does not trigger.
Test that BMS_InsulationFault_Flag is triggered when measured isolation resistance < r_Insulation_MinThreshold and both main contactors are opened.
Verify the latched state of BMS_InsulationFault_Latch persists for 1 second after BMS_InsulationFault_Flag is set, then clears upon diagnostic clear.
Ensure that BMS_InsulationFault_Flag and BMS_InsulationFault_Latch are correctly observed in the system logs during fault conditions.

description:
IF  any cell temperature <= low temperature limit
THEN
  · BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1
  · BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
supplementary_info:
Function: Thermal Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection
Context: Low temperature protection prevents charging when cells are too cold to accept current without damage, and manages the battery heater to enable charging under cold conditions. The heater is de-energized on overtemperature or when no longer needed:
- BMS_CellUT_Flag = 1: any cell <= low temperature limit (charging prohibited, BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 0)
- BMS_HeaterOT_Flag = 1: heater temperature exceeds safe limit
- Heater activation threshold: cell <= -10 C during charge session

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1 when any cell temperature <= low temperature limit; clearing: BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Test the fault scenario where a cell temperature is significantly below the expected operating range, verifying BMS response and charging prohibition.
Test the state transition from normal operation to under-temperature protection mode when any cell temperature drops below the limit.
Verify that the system correctly updates BMS_CellUT_Flag and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag in response to changes in cell temperatures during a charge session.

description: A current sensor self-test shall be performed during each power-up initialisation. The test shall inject a known reference and verify within 2 % accuracy.
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault
Context: Current sensor plausibility is verified against a model-predicted value based on pack voltage slope and operating mode. A sensor fault triggers degraded operation using voltage-based power estimation. Sensor self-test is performed during each power-up initialisation (see section 13):
- BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 1: sensor deviation > 10 % from prediction
- BMS_DeratePower_Req = 1: request VCU to reduce power demand
- Fault persisting > 5 s triggers BMS_SafeState_Entry = 1

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 1 when sensor deviation > 10 % from prediction; clearing: BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 0 when sensor deviation < 10 % from prediction.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Verify that the current sensor self-test is performed during each power-up initialization and BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag is set to 1 if the deviation from the predicted value exceeds 10%.
Test the boundary condition where the sensor deviation is exactly 10% from the prediction, ensuring that BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag remains at 0.
Simulate a scenario where the current sensor fails and verify that BMS_DeratePower_Req = 1 and BMS_SafeState_Entry = 1 after the fault persists for more than 5 seconds.
Test the state transition from normal operation to degraded operation when BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag is set due to a sensor deviation greater than 10%.
Verify that the current sensor self-test results are observable through the BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag signal during power-up initialization.
